rulings.Case Title: YOLANDA MIANI, ET AL VS. LABIB MAJID BARANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Contempt And Req. For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFFS YOLANDA MIANI, CESAR MIANI'S Notice Of Motion For Contempt And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance is required. Defendants are to appear and provide proof of inability to pay the outstanding $1,550 sanction and provide a feasible schedule for payments (e.g., $300 today and an amount certain in the next 12 months.) =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: SYDNEY GUREWITZ CLEMENS VS. CONNOR COCHRAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Ntc Of Continued Motion And Motion To Strike Request For Trial De Novo Of Deft Conlan Press, Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 22, 2012, Line 10, PLAINTIFF SYDNEY GUREWITZ CLEMENS' Re-noticed Motion To Strike Request For Trial De Novo Of Defendant Conlan Press, Inc. Continued to December 3, 2012 to give defendant an opportunity to pay its back taxes and obtain a certificate of revivor from the Secretary of State. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID DEFFNER VS. LEILA S. KERIMOVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Deem Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, PLAINTIFF DAVID DEFFNER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Deem Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. No opposition was filed. Moving party shall provide the Court with a proposed order if it has not been provided with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ALIX SANTA-IGLESIA et al VS. BALWANTSINH THAKOR, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And His Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 5, PLAINTIFFS ALIX SANTA-IGLESIA, DEBORAH BASS, DANIEL LEE, WILLIAM CABRAL, CLYDE ROSE, JAMES HARRIS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And His Counsel Of Record Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. Plaintiff's discovery seeks relevant information that tracks the allegations in Plaintiffs' complaint. Plaintiffs and defendants request for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. No opposition was filed. Moving party shall provide the Court with a proposed order if it has not been provided with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DECASTRO VS. HERBALIFE INTERNATIONAL, INC., A NEVADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Respnoses From Det'S Special Interrogatories, Set No. One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 9, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL DECASTRO'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set No. One Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties are to appear for argument. Plaintiff should be prepared to explain why he is seeking information on products after plaintiff stopped taking a specific Herbalife product. Plaintiff should also explain why plaintiff is seeking information on products plaintiff never consumed or that were not sold in the U.S. =302/JPT
